title: erica erigena f. alba
intro: Erica erigena f. alba, also known as the White Heather, is a subspecies of the Erica erigena plant, a flowering shrub native to Ireland and the UK. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Erica erigena f. alba include reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and aiding in the healing of cuts and scrapes. Herbal preparations of White Heather include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to relieve symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
